title: Amendment to COB 2.3.2 – Disclosure requirements for retail clients
snippet: The disclosure requirements for retail clients have been updated to include clear summary of costs and charges in a standardized format before execution.
fullText: The DFSA has amended COB 2.3.2 (Disclosure to Retail Clients) with effect from 1 February 2025. Authorised Firms must provide retail clients with a clear, fair and not misleading summary of all costs and charges in a standardized format before the client is bound by any agreement. This includes one-off costs, recurring costs, and any third-party charges. The format must be consistent with the template published in the DFSA Rulebook Appendix.

title: GEN 3.2.1 – Updated capital adequacy reporting frequency
snippet: Reporting frequency for capital adequacy has been revised from quarterly to monthly for certain categories of Authorised Firms.
fullText: GEN 3.2.1 has been updated to require monthly submission of capital adequacy returns for Authorised Firms classified as Category 1 or 2. The first monthly return under the new rule is due for the period ending 31 March 2025. Firms must use the revised Form CA-1 available on the DFSA portal. Waivers may be applied for in exceptional circumstances.

title: SAMA Circular on Cybersecurity Framework for Licensed Institutions
snippet: New cybersecurity requirements for SAMA-licensed institutions including incident reporting timelines and resilience testing.
fullText: SAMA has issued a circular (Ref: 123/2025) requiring all licensed institutions to align with the updated Cybersecurity Framework by 30 June 2025. Key requirements include: (1) Notification of significant cyber incidents to SAMA within 4 hours of detection; (2) Annual penetration testing by independent parties; (3) Board-level reporting on cyber risk at least twice yearly; (4) Minimum baseline controls as set out in the Framework annex. Non-compliance may result in supervisory action.

title: Amendment to Open Banking Standards
snippet: Technical standards for open banking APIs have been updated to support new account types and consent management.
fullText: SAMA has published an amendment to the Open Banking Technical Standards (v2.1) effective 15 March 2025. Changes include: support for SME and corporate accounts in the Account Information and Payment Initiation APIs; mandatory use of the revised consent management flow; and updated security requirements for strong customer authentication. Participants must complete certification against the new standards by 15 May 2025.

title: CMA Rules on ESG Disclosure for Listed Companies
snippet: New rules require listed companies to disclose environmental, social and governance (ESG) information in annual reports.
fullText: The Capital Market Authority has issued rules requiring all listed companies to include ESG disclosures in their annual reports for financial years beginning on or after 1 January 2025. Disclosures must cover: governance structure for sustainability; environmental metrics (emissions, energy, water); social metrics (workforce, community); and alignment with Saudi Green Initiative where applicable. A transition period applies for smaller issuers until 2026.

title: Updated Offer Document Requirements for Sukuk Issuances
snippet: Offer document and prospectus requirements for sukuk issuances have been revised to enhance investor disclosure.
fullText: The CMA has revised the Offer of Securities Regulations with respect to sukuk. Key changes: (1) Offer documents must include a clear description of the Shari'ah structure and relevant resolutions; (2) Risk factors must be presented in order of materiality; (3) Minimum content for profit distribution mechanics; (4) Updated template for summary terms. Applies to all sukuk offers submitted from 1 April 2025.

title: Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan – Phase 1 Implementation Guidelines
snippet: Implementation guidelines for the first phase of Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan have been published, focusing on sustainable development zones.
fullText: The Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan Phase 1 Implementation Guidelines set out how government and private sector should align projects with the plan's five pillars: sustainable development, mobility, quality of life, economic diversification, and heritage. Key deliverables for Phase 1 (2025–2027) include zoning updates for designated development zones, green building standards alignment, and transport corridor planning. All new major developments must demonstrate alignment with the plan from Q2 2025.

title: Update to Dubai 2040 Environmental Sustainability Targets
snippet: Revised targets for green space, renewable energy share, and carbon reduction in line with UAE Net Zero 2050.
fullText: Dubai 2040 has been updated to align environmental targets with UAE Net Zero 2050. Revisions include: increase in per-capita green space target; commitment to 50% renewable energy in the emirate's mix by 2030; and carbon reduction milestones for the built environment. Regulators will incorporate these into permitting and approval criteria. The update was approved by the Supreme Committee in January 2025.

title: Vision 2030 – Updated Local Content and Saudization Requirements
snippet: Revised local content (Nusaned) and Saudization thresholds for certain sectors under Vision 2030.
fullText: The Vision 2030 implementation office has published updated local content and Saudization requirements effective from 1 March 2025. Changes include: higher local content thresholds for energy, construction and manufacturing in government and semi-government tenders; updated Nitaqat bands and Saudization percentages for retail and hospitality; and new incentives for training and upskilling Saudi nationals. Contractors and suppliers must ensure compliance for new contracts.

title: Vision 2030 – Tourism and Heritage Sector Regulatory Update
snippet: New regulatory framework for tourism and heritage projects to support Vision 2030 tourism targets.
fullText: A new regulatory framework for tourism and heritage projects has been issued under Vision 2030. It covers licensing, zoning, and quality standards for tourism developments, including giga-projects. Requirements include environmental impact assessments, heritage impact assessments where applicable, and alignment with national tourism strategy KPIs. The framework is effective for new project approvals from 1 April 2025.

title: SDAIA National Data Governance Framework – Amendment
snippet: Amendments to the National Data Governance Framework covering cross-border data transfer and AI training data.
fullText: SDAIA has amended the National Data Governance Framework (NDGF) with effect from 1 February 2025. Key amendments: (1) Clarified rules for cross-border transfer of non-personal data for AI training, subject to adequacy or safeguards; (2) New categories for government data classification; (3) Requirements for data quality and documentation when data is used for AI systems; (4) Alignment with the Personal Data Protection Law. Entities under the NDGF must review and update policies by 1 June 2025.

title: SDAIA AI Ethics Guidelines – Update for Public Sector
snippet: Updated AI ethics guidelines for public sector use of artificial intelligence, including transparency and accountability.
fullText: SDAIA has published an update to the AI Ethics Guidelines specifically for public sector entities. The update emphasizes: (1) Transparency in automated decision-making affecting individuals; (2) Human oversight and appeal mechanisms; (3) Documentation and audit trails for high-impact AI systems; (4) Bias and fairness assessments before deployment. Public entities must conduct a gap assessment against the guidelines by 30 September 2025 and report progress to SDAIA.

title: FSRA amendments to client asset and custody rules
snippet: ADGM FSRA has updated the Client Assets and Client Money rules with enhanced segregation and reporting requirements.
fullText: The Financial Services Regulatory Authority of ADGM has amended the Client Assets and Client Money module with effect from 1 February 2025. Key changes: (1) Enhanced segregation requirements for client money; (2) Monthly reconciliation and reporting to the FSRA; (3) Independent assurance reports for designated firms. Authorised firms must comply by 31 May 2025.

title: AML/CFT annual return and risk assessment updates
snippet: FSRA has revised the AML/CFT annual return form and clarified risk assessment expectations for ADGM firms.
fullText: ADGM FSRA has issued an updated AML/CFT annual return template and guidance on firm-wide risk assessments. Firms must submit the revised return for the period ending 31 December 2024 by 31 March 2025. The guidance emphasizes board-level ownership of AML risk and documented risk-based approach.

title: Amendment to beneficial ownership disclosure requirements
snippet: ADGM Companies Regulations have been updated to align beneficial ownership disclosure with UAE federal AML requirements.
fullText: The ADGM Companies Regulations have been amended to require registered entities to maintain and file beneficial ownership information in line with UAE Federal Decree-Law on AML/CFT. The Registrar will issue updated forms and submission deadlines. Effective for all entities from 1 April 2025.

title: CBUAE circular on stablecoin and virtual asset regulations
snippet: Central Bank of the UAE has issued guidance on regulation of stablecoins and virtual asset activities by licensed institutions.
fullText: The CBUAE has published a circular (Circular No. 12/2025) setting out the regulatory approach to stablecoins and virtual asset activities. Licensed financial institutions must seek prior approval for any virtual asset-related products or services. The circular also clarifies applicability of federal AML/CFT requirements to virtual asset service providers. Compliance date: 30 June 2025.

title: Updated large exposure and concentration risk limits
snippet: CBUAE has revised the large exposure and concentration risk framework for banks and finance companies.
fullText: The Central Bank of the UAE has amended the large exposure standards in the Prudential Regulations. New single-counterparty and group exposure limits apply from 1 March 2025. Banks must report current exposures and submit implementation plans by 28 February 2025.

title: Federal Decree-Law No. 10 of 2025 – AML/CFT and proliferation financing
snippet: UAE federal AML/CFT law updated to include proliferation financing and enhanced beneficial ownership obligations.
fullText: Federal Decree-Law No. 10 of 2025 regarding Anti-Money Laundering, Combating the Financing of Terrorism and Proliferation Financing has been promulgated. Key changes: (1) Proliferation financing brought into federal law; (2) Stricter beneficial ownership disclosure; (3) Virtual asset service providers in scope; (4) Enhanced penalties and regulator powers. The law came into force on 14 October 2025; designated entities must align policies and procedures by dates set by the supervisory authority.

title: Commercial Companies Law – amendments to branch and representative office rules
snippet: Amendments to UAE Commercial Companies Law affecting branch and representative office registration and reporting.
fullText: The Ministry of Economy has issued amendments to the implementing regulations of the Commercial Companies Law relating to branches and representative offices of foreign companies. New registration and annual reporting requirements apply from 1 April 2025. Existing branches must comply by 30 June 2025.

title: JAFZA licensing and renewal process digitization
snippet: Jebel Ali Free Zone has updated the licensing and renewal process with full digital submission requirements.
fullText: JAFZA has announced the migration to a fully digital licensing and renewal process effective 1 March 2025. All applications and renewals must be submitted through the designated portal. Paper submissions will no longer be accepted after 28 February 2025. Supporting document requirements have been updated in the Operating Regulations.

title: DMCC company formation and disclosure updates
snippet: DMCC has updated company formation requirements and annual disclosure obligations for registered entities.
fullText: The Dubai Multi Commodities Centre has amended its Company Regulations to align with federal requirements and streamline formation. Key updates: (1) Revised minimum capital and disclosure for certain activities; (2) Annual confirmation of beneficial ownership; (3) Updated compliance declaration. Effective for new incorporations from 1 April 2025 and for existing companies from next renewal.

title: DMCC AML/CFT policy and procedure template update
snippet: DMCC has published an updated AML/CFT policy and procedure template for commodity and trading members.
fullText: DMCC has issued an updated AML/CFT policy and procedure template reflecting UAE federal law and best practice. All designated members must review and, where necessary, update their policies by 31 May 2025. The template covers risk assessment, customer due diligence, record-keeping and reporting.

title: QFCRA rules on investment funds and custody
snippet: Qatar Financial Centre Regulatory Authority has amended the Collective Investment Rules and custody requirements.
fullText: The QFCRA has amended the Collective Investment Rules and related custody provisions with effect from 15 February 2025. Changes include: (1) Updated eligibility criteria for fund managers; (2) Enhanced custody and depositary requirements; (3) Reporting timelines. QFC-authorised firms must comply by 30 June 2025.

title: Conduct of business – client classification and suitability
snippet: QFCRA has updated conduct of business rules on client classification and suitability assessments.
fullText: The QFCRA has revised the Conduct of Business module regarding client classification and suitability. The new rules require documented reassessment of client classification when circumstances change and enhanced suitability reports for advised sales. Effective 1 April 2025.

title: Qatar AML/CFT law – implementing regulations update
snippet: Implementing regulations under the Qatar AML/CFT law have been updated with new reporting and record-keeping requirements.
fullText: The Qatar National Anti-Money Laundering and Combating the Financing of Terrorism Committee has issued updated implementing regulations. Key changes: (1) Extended record-keeping period for certain categories; (2) Revised suspicious transaction reporting form and timeline; (3) Alignment with FATF recommendations. Designated entities must comply by 1 July 2025.

title: CBB volume 7 (Capital Markets) – amendments
snippet: Central Bank of Bahrain has amended Volume 7 (Capital Markets) of the CBB Rulebook.
fullText: The CBB has issued amendments to Volume 7 (Capital Markets) of the CBB Rulebook effective 1 March 2025. Changes cover disclosure requirements for listed issuers, market conduct and reporting. All CBB-licensed capital market participants must comply with the revised modules by the effective date.

title: CBB AML/CFT module update
snippet: Central Bank of Bahrain has updated the AML/CFT module with enhanced CDD and STR requirements.
fullText: The CBB has revised the Anti-Money Laundering and Combating the Financing of Terrorism module. Key updates: (1) Enhanced customer due diligence for high-risk jurisdictions; (2) Revised suspicious transaction reporting timelines; (3) Board and senior management responsibilities. Licensees must update policies and procedures by 30 June 2025.

title: Bahrain Bourse ESG disclosure guidance for listed companies
snippet: Bahrain Bourse has published ESG disclosure guidance for listed companies.
fullText: Bahrain Bourse has issued guidance on environmental, social and governance (ESG) disclosures for listed companies. The guidance recommends disclosure of governance structure, environmental metrics and social indicators in line with regional and international practice. Listed issuers are encouraged to adopt the guidance for reporting periods starting from 1 January 2025.

title: Oman CMA corporate governance code update
snippet: Oman Capital Market Authority has updated the corporate governance code for listed companies.
fullText: The Oman CMA has issued an updated Corporate Governance Code for listed companies effective for financial years beginning on or after 1 January 2025. The code covers board composition, committees, disclosure and shareholder rights. Listed companies must disclose compliance in their annual reports.

title: Oman AML/CFT law – amendments and supervisory circular
snippet: Oman has amended the AML/CFT law and the supervisory authority has issued an implementation circular.
fullText: Amendments to the Sultanate of Oman AML/CFT law have been promulgated, and the competent authority has issued a circular on implementation. Key areas: (1) Designated non-financial businesses and professions; (2) Virtual assets; (3) Penalties and enforcement. Regulated entities must review and update compliance frameworks by 30 September 2025.

title: Kuwait CMA disclosure and listing rules amendment
snippet: Kuwait Capital Markets Authority has amended disclosure and listing rules for listed companies.
fullText: The Kuwait CMA has issued amendments to the disclosure and listing rules. Changes include: (1) Quarterly and annual disclosure timelines; (2) Material event disclosure; (3) Related-party transaction approval and disclosure. The amendments are effective from 1 April 2025.

title: Kuwait AML/CFT law – implementing regulations
snippet: Implementing regulations under the Kuwait AML/CFT law have been updated.
fullText: The Kuwait Anti-Money Laundering and Combating the Financing of Terrorism law's implementing regulations have been updated. Designated entities must align policies, procedures and reporting with the new requirements. The supervisory authority has set a compliance deadline of 31 August 2025.
